what_it_does: This file is an IIFE browser module that exposes window.forkVBrain.attach. It builds a self-contained brain UI inside a host panel element, reads a live aim address/label from the host, and wires a textarea/send button to three operations: a blocking grounded question (/api/brain/ask), an optional streaming Claude Code turn (via window.forkBrainCore.talk), an open channel post (/api/channel/post), and a direction routed back at the current aim (via core.writeDirections). It renders all results into its own .v-brain-reply slot and hides raw addresses from the operator.
observations: Depends on a global window.forkBrainCore being loaded first
Builds UI idempotently only if .v-brain is not already present inside panelEl
Uses CSS classes prefixed with v-brain-* intended to be re-skinned by DNA
The default send button and Enter key fire groundedAsk, not the streaming ask
ask() reads the aim address at send-time so re-aiming between turns changes context automatically
groundedAsk is blocking and shows a '…' placeholder while waiting
postToChannel translates backend error strings into human-readable messages
direct() merges caller-provided item fields into an element_id + annotation_type: 'direction' payload
Shift+Enter allows newlines in the textarea
standouts: Dual brain paths (streaming Claude Code vs blocking grounded mind) co-located in one UI module with a single reply renderer
Operator-law enforcement: raw aim addresses are never shown; only a human label is rendered
Default V query path is the grounded mind (L2) rather than the streaming agent
postToChannel performs keyword-based error translation to keep failures human-readable
conventions: IIFE with a single namespace export
Live value reading at action time rather than snapshotting on attach
Fail-soft rendering with human-readable fallback text
concerns: Relies on a global window.forkBrainCore without any local fallback or import contract
No cancellation or cleanup of in-flight fetch requests when destroy() is called
groundedAsk and ask both write to the same replyEl; interleaving could clobber output
postToChannel error translation is substring-based and could misclassify novel backend messages
No visible input sanitization or length limits before sending to backend routes
direct() trusts the caller's item shape and does not validate fields
notes: A mountable right-hand-man brain controller that grounds questions at the current aim, can post to channels, and routes directions back to the aimed element, while keeping raw addresses hidden from the operator.
questions: What exact shape does forkBrainCore.talk expect and return?
How is the streaming content rendered into replyEl during ask()?
What fields are valid inside the item object passed to direct()?
How does the backend resolve a human channel name in postToChannel?
Is ask() intended to be wired to a separate UI trigger, or is it currently unused?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a persistent, per-page, aim-aware V brain module that binds talk/post/direct to the current aim and hides machine names from the operator.
apparent_themes: Operator-law: show meaning, not machine identifiers
Aim-centric context: the brain loads at whatever address the V currently points at
Single-source rendering: this module owns its panel reply slot
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a reusable, human-facing 'right-hand-man' interface that can be dropped onto any page, always know what the user is looking at, answer questions through either a fast grounded mind or a streaming agent, post openly to named channels, and send directions back into the surface without exposing implementation details.
novelty: It combines a streaming Claude Code path and a blocking local grounded-mind path in the same attachable module, defaulting the everyday ask button to the grounded mind while keeping the streaming path available for future use.
contribution: A self-contained attachable V-brain controller that renders its own panel, grounds queries to a live aim, exposes both streaming and blocking query modes, posts to channels with humanized error feedback, and routes directions back to the current aim.
summary_for_embedding: An IIFE browser module that attaches a self-rendering V brain panel to a host element, binding queries, channel posts, and directions to a live aim address via a global forkBrainCore.
intention_for_embedding: To give every page a persistent, aim-aware right-hand-man interface that lets an operator ask, post, and direct without seeing raw addresses.

symbols.does: Mounts the V brain UI into a host panel and returns an API for ask, groundedAsk, postToChannel, direct, aimChanged, and destroy.
symbols.description: Validates panelEl and getAimAddress, builds the .v-brain DOM if absent, binds the send button and Enter key to groundedAsk, and exposes the module's public surface. It is the single entry point used by projection's V-overlay host.

symbols.does: Returns the human-readable label for the current aim, falling back to 'this'.
symbols.description: Wraps the optional getAimLabel callback safely so the UI never displays a raw address. If the callback throws or is absent, it returns the generic fallback.

symbols.does: Updates the .v-brain-aim element to show 'Ask about: <human label>'.
symbols.description: Refreshes the visible aim meaning whenever the host signals an aim change, enforcing the operator-law that only human labels are shown.

symbols.does: Sends a streaming Claude Code turn to the current aim via forkBrainCore.talk.
symbols.description: Reads the live aim address, clears the input, and delegates streaming rendering to the global core. It is exported but not wired to the default send button, reserved for a future streaming/agent entry point.

symbols.does: Sends a blocking grounded-mind question to /api/brain/ask and self-renders the answer.
symbols.description: Shows a '…' placeholder, POSTs the question with optional aim, then renders the stripped answer or a clear failure note into .v-brain-reply. Also returns the parsed response for callers that want to chain.

symbols.does: Maps raw backend post_to_channel error strings to short human phrases.
symbols.description: Performs substring matching on the error message to classify failures as 'that channel is closed', 'that channel has no one in it yet', 'I couldn’t find that channel', 'there was nothing to post', or a generic fallback.

symbols.does: Posts a message to a named channel via /api/channel/post and renders a transparency line.
symbols.description: Renders in-flight and result states into .v-brain-reply, returning the parsed response. On failure it uses _humanPostError so the operator sees only human meaning, never raw backend error text.

symbols.does: Routes a direction/comment/reaction back at the current aim through core.writeDirections.
symbols.description: Reads the live aim address, wraps the caller-provided item with element_id and annotation_type: 'direction', and forwards it to the brain core for persistence/re-render.

symbols.does: Removes the .v-brain root from the host panel.
symbols.description: Simple DOM teardown; it does not cancel in-flight requests or remove event listeners from the now-detached elements.
